disclaimer: Curated operator procedures for enhanced OEM functions that auto-architect cannot execute over standard OBD-II (Mode 01–07). Steps cite public owner/tech reports; verify against a dealer tool or AlfaOBD prompts on the vehicle. Do not invent bi-directional gateway commands from this catalog.

procedures.title: Proxi alignment (module configuration sync)
procedures.summary: On FCA next-generation electrical architecture (Jeep Renegade and related), the Body Control Module (BCM) is the master of Proxi configuration data. After a dead battery, battery disconnect, or certain module/radio/switch changes, other ECUs can lose their handshake with the BCM. The vehicle may stay in Park, show a flashing odometer, and light ABS/traction or service warnings. A Proxi (proxy) alignment re-syncs modules to the BCM. Standard Mode 03 OBD scans alone are not enough — you need an enhanced tool (AlfaOBD or dealer wiTECH) plus OBDLink MX+, typically with the gray OBD adapter during the routine.
triggers: Vehicle starts but will not shift out of Park (or shifter lock bypass does not restore drive)
Flashing odometer after battery disconnect, battery death, or module/radio work
ABS / traction control lights and/or cluster service messages after battery work
Enhanced scan shows configuration-mismatch / Proxi-related faults (e.g. TCM vs BCM sync loss; related FCA reports cite P1500-00 ECU configuration mismatch on sibling platforms)

modulesInvolved.role: Master of Proxi configuration — alignment is run from the body computer / BCM
Often loses Proxi sync after battery events; common reason the vehicle stays in Park
May show traction/ABS warnings when out of sync with BCM Proxi data
Instrument cluster / odometer flashing is a classic Proxi-mismatch symptom
Radio, steering, and other modules may participate; a full-module enhanced scan lists which ECUs report mismatch

detectSteps: Confirm recent battery disconnect/death, jump-start, or module/accessory work (radio, switches) — common Proxi triggers on 2015 Renegade (no Security Gateway on this year).
Note cabin symptoms: stuck in Park, flashing odometer, ABS/traction or service lamps. These suggest Proxi mismatch but do not prove it by themselves.
Do a standard OBD scan (this app / MX+ Mode 03–07) and record any powertrain codes — useful context, but insufficient alone for Proxi diagnosis.

With an enhanced tool (AlfaOBD or dealer wiTECH), scan all modules — not only the PCM. Look for configuration-mismatch / Proxi-related DTCs and which ECU reports them (TCM, ABS, IPC, etc.). The BCM remains the Proxi master; mismatch codes on other modules identify who is out of sync with the BCM.

If the enhanced tool reports Proxi/configuration mismatch (or classic symptom cluster after battery work with no mechanical Park-lock failure), proceed to alignment. If a module is offline/no-comm, fix network/power/module hardware before or instead of alignment.
alignSteps: Hardware: OBDLink MX+ (or compatible ELM327-class interface supported by AlfaOBD) and the gray OBD-II adapter/extension used between the vehicle DLC and the MX+ when AlfaOBD prompts for it. 2015 Renegade: no Security Gateway bypass required.
Install AlfaOBD (phone or PC). Pair/connect the MX+ so AlfaOBD can talk to the vehicle.
In AlfaOBD select: Jeep → Renegade → Body computer (BCM).
Connect to the body computer, then choose PROXI alignment (wording may be PROXI / Proxy alignment).

Start the procedure and follow on-screen prompts exactly. Typical Renegade flow reported by operators: procedure starts → turn ignition/key OFF → plug in the gray adapter with the OBDLink on the end → turn ignition/key back ON → wait until the tool reports Finished.
When Finished, start the engine. Do not clear codes as a substitute for completing the alignment.
Dealer alternative: wiTECH (or equivalent) Proxi/configuration alignment from the BCM — same intent, different UI.
verifySteps: Confirm the vehicle shifts out of Park into Reverse/Drive (safe area, brake applied).
Confirm the odometer is no longer flashing and ABS/traction/service warnings related to the mismatch have cleared or can be cleared after a successful re-scan.
Re-scan all modules with the enhanced tool — configuration-mismatch / Proxi DTCs should be gone. Re-scan standard OBD in this app for remaining powertrain codes.
In auto-architect Functions, mark the guided run completed (or failed with notes) so the Journal records the outcome.
hardware: Gray-type OBD-II adapter/extension between DLC and MX+ (required when AlfaOBD prompts; used for Proxi on Renegade)
AlfaOBD license/app, or dealer wiTECH session
risks: This app cannot send Proxi alignment over the standard OBD gateway — execution is external (AlfaOBD/wiTECH).
A bad or DOA gray adapter cable will fail mid-procedure; verify continuity if the tool stalls after the adapter prompt.
Proxi will not fix a failed shifter mechanism, dead TCM, or open CAN/LIN wiring — rule those out if alignment completes but Park lock remains.
Do not invent AT/UDS command sequences; follow the enhanced tool’s prompts.
references: Owner/tech reports: FCA next-gen BCM Proxi after battery disconnect (Renegade / Cherokee / 500X class) — stuck in Park, flashing odometer, ABS lights
AlfaOBD Renegade path: Body computer → PROXI alignment; gray adapter prompt mid-procedure (Jeep Renegade Forum operator reports)
Related FCA configuration mismatch example discussed publicly: P1500-00 ECU configuration mismatch (TCM) after battery replacement on sibling platforms — verify exact code text on your enhanced scan
